
# VENDOR MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

**Contract Reference:** COHERON-MSA-[YEAR]-[SEQ]
**Version:** 1.0
**Generated by:** CoheronConnect Legal Module · Coheron Tech Private Limited · Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
**Classification:** Confidential

---

## PARTIES

This Master Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of **[EFFECTIVE_DATE]** ("Effective Date")

**BETWEEN:**

**1. [PROVIDER_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, bearing CIN **[PROVIDER_CIN]**, GSTIN **[PROVIDER_GSTIN]**, PAN **[PROVIDER_PAN]**, having its registered office at **[PROVIDER_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Service Provider" or "Provider");

**AND**

**2. [CLIENT_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, [CLIENT_INCORPORATION_YEAR], bearing CIN **[CLIENT_CIN]**, GSTIN **[CLIENT_GSTIN]**, PAN **[CLIENT_PAN]**, having its registered office at **[CLIENT_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Client").

Provider and Client are each a "Party" and collectively the "Parties."

---

## RECITALS

(A) Provider is engaged in the business of providing technology and professional services, including software development, IT consulting, managed services, and related support.

(B) Client wishes to engage Provider to provide certain services, and Provider is willing to provide such services, on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement.

(C) This Agreement establishes the master terms and conditions governing all services engagements between the Parties. Individual projects and engagements shall be governed by Statements of Work executed under this Agreement.

**NOW THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual promises and covenants set out herein, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## 1. DEFINITIONS

**1.1 "Agreement"** means this Master Services Agreement together with all Schedules, Statements of Work, and amendments executed hereunder.

**1.2 "Confidential Information"** means any information, data, or material — in any form — disclosed by one Party to the other in connection with this Agreement that is marked confidential or would reasonably be understood to be confidential.

**1.3 "Deliverable"** means any work product, software, documentation, report, or other output that Provider is required to produce and deliver to Client under a Statement of Work.

**1.4 "Intellectual Property Rights"** means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, service marks, trade secrets, database rights, design rights, moral rights, and all other intellectual property rights of any kind, whether registered or unregistered, anywhere in the world.

**1.5 "Key Personnel"** means the specific named individuals identified in a Statement of Work as critical to the performance of the Services.

**1.6 "Personal Data"** has the meaning assigned under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.

**1.7 "Services"** means the technology, professional, and support services that Provider agrees to provide to Client under each Statement of Work.

**1.8 "Service Fees"** means the fees payable by Client to Provider for the Services as specified in each Statement of Work.

**1.9 "Statement of Work" or "SOW"** means a written order or scope document executed by both Parties that references this Agreement and sets out the specific services, deliverables, timelines, fees, and other particulars for an individual engagement.

**1.10 "Dispute"** means any dispute, difference, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, including its formation, validity, performance, breach, or termination.

---

## 2. SCOPE OF SERVICES

**2.1** Provider shall provide the Services as described in each SOW executed under this Agreement. Each SOW is incorporated into and forms part of this Agreement.

**2.2** In the event of any conflict between the terms of this Agreement and any SOW, the terms of the SOW shall prevail with respect to that specific engagement only, unless the SOW expressly states otherwise.

**2.3** Provider shall perform the Services: (a) with reasonable skill, care, and diligence; (b) in accordance with applicable industry standards and best practices; (c) using suitably qualified and experienced personnel; and (d) in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

**2.4** Provider shall promptly notify Client of any matter that may materially affect the delivery of Services, including changes in resource availability, anticipated delays, or technical risks.

---

## 3. STATEMENTS OF WORK

**3.1** Each SOW shall specify: (a) a description of the Services to be performed; (b) Deliverables and acceptance criteria; (c) project timeline and milestones; (d) Service Fees and payment schedule; (e) Key Personnel (if applicable); (f) any Client responsibilities and dependencies; and (g) any special terms applicable to that engagement.

**3.2** An SOW shall be binding only when executed in writing by authorised representatives of both Parties.

**3.3** Neither Party is obligated to enter into any SOW. This Agreement does not guarantee any minimum volume of business to Provider.

---

## 4. CLIENT RESPONSIBILITIES

**4.1** Client shall: (a) provide timely access to systems, data, facilities, and personnel reasonably required for Provider to perform the Services; (b) make decisions and provide approvals within the timeframes set out in the relevant SOW; (c) ensure that all information provided to Provider is accurate and complete; and (d) designate a Client project manager as the primary point of contact.

**4.2** Provider shall not be liable for delays or failures in performance to the extent caused by Client's failure to fulfil its obligations under this clause.

---

## 5. FEES AND PAYMENT

**5.1 Service Fees.** Client shall pay Provider the Service Fees set out in each SOW.

**5.2 Invoicing.** Provider shall issue valid tax invoices in the format prescribed under GST law, including the relevant HSN/SAC code, applicable GST rate, and Provider's GSTIN.

**5.3 Payment Terms.** Unless otherwise specified in the SOW, payment is due within **thirty (30) calendar days** from the date of invoice ("Due Date").

**5.4 Late Payment.** Amounts not paid by the Due Date shall accrue interest at the rate of **1.5% per month** (or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, if lower) from the Due Date until the date of actual payment, compounded monthly.

**5.5 Disputed Invoices.** If Client disputes any invoice in good faith, Client shall: (a) notify Provider in writing within ten (10) business days of receipt, specifying the disputed amount and the basis of dispute; (b) pay the undisputed portion by the Due Date; and (c) cooperate in good faith to resolve the dispute within fifteen (15) business days of the dispute notice.

**5.6 Expenses.** Unless the SOW expressly states otherwise, all expenses are included in the Service Fees. Where pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses are reimbursable, they shall be invoiced at cost with supporting documentation.

---

## 6. TAXES AND GST (M-4)

**6.1 Exclusive of Tax.** All Service Fees are exclusive of GST and all other applicable taxes, levies, duties, cesses, and surcharges.

**6.2 GST.** GST at the applicable rate shall be charged additionally per the CGST Act, 2017, IGST Act, 2017, and applicable State GST Act. Each party shall provide valid tax invoices in the format prescribed under GST law.

**6.3 TDS.** Where Client is required to deduct tax at source under the Income Tax Act, 1961, Client shall: (a) deduct TDS at the applicable rate at the time of credit or payment, whichever is earlier; (b) deposit the deducted amount with the tax authority within the prescribed time; (c) issue a valid TDS certificate (Form 16A or applicable form) to Provider within the prescribed period; and (d) file required TDS returns within prescribed due dates. Provider shall be entitled to receive the full contracted fee, with TDS deducted and deposited on Provider's behalf.

**6.4 Input Tax Credit.** Each party is responsible for its own GST compliance and shall ensure timely filing of returns to facilitate input tax credit for the other party.

**6.5 Currency.** All Service Fees shall be denominated and paid in Indian Rupees (INR). All payments shall be made by electronic transfer (NEFT/RTGS/IMPS) to the bank account designated by Provider. Where foreign exchange conversion is applicable, conversion shall be at the RBI reference rate on the date of invoice.

---

## 7. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

**7.1 Provider Background IP.** All intellectual property rights in Provider's pre-existing tools, frameworks, methodologies, libraries, and know-how ("Background IP") remain the exclusive property of Provider. Provider grants Client a non-exclusive, non-transferable, royalty-free licence to use Background IP solely to the extent embedded in or necessary to use the Deliverables.

**7.2 Client Background IP.** All intellectual property rights in Client's pre-existing materials, data, and systems ("Client Background IP") remain the exclusive property of Client.

**7.3 Deliverables IP.** Unless otherwise agreed in writing in the relevant SOW, all Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement, excluding Provider Background IP, shall be **works made for hire** and all intellectual property rights therein shall vest exclusively in Client upon full payment of all outstanding Service Fees.

**7.4 Licence to Deliverables.** Until full payment is received, Provider grants Client a limited, revocable licence to use Deliverables solely for the purposes set out in the SOW.

---

## 8. IP INDEMNIFICATION (M-7)

**8.1 Provider Indemnity.** Provider shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Client and its officers, directors, and employees from and against any third-party claims alleging that the Services or Deliverables, as provided and used in accordance with this Agreement, infringe any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret rights recognised under Indian law. Provider shall pay all damages, costs, and reasonable legal fees finally awarded by a competent court or agreed in a settlement approved in writing by Provider.

**8.2 Remediation.** If any Services or Deliverables are or are likely to become the subject of an IP infringement claim, Provider may, at its sole option: (a) procure for Client the right to continue using the Services or Deliverables; (b) modify the Services or Deliverables to make them non-infringing while maintaining substantially equivalent functionality; (c) replace the Services or Deliverables with a non-infringing equivalent of substantially equivalent functionality; or (d) if none of the foregoing is commercially practicable, terminate the affected SOW and refund to Client pre-paid fees for the unexpired period.

**8.3 Exclusions.** Provider has no indemnification obligation for claims arising from: (a) unauthorised modification of the Services or Deliverables by Client or any third party; (b) combination of the Services or Deliverables with products, data, or services not provided or approved by Provider; (c) Client's failure to use a non-infringing update or replacement provided by Provider; or (d) use of the Services or Deliverables in a manner not permitted by this Agreement.

**8.4 Process.** As a condition of indemnification, Client shall: (a) promptly notify Provider in writing of any claim (delay that prejudices Provider's defence forfeits indemnity rights); (b) grant Provider sole control of the defence and settlement, provided no settlement may be entered that imposes liability or obligations on Client without Client's prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld); and (c) provide Provider all reasonable assistance at Provider's expense.

---

## 9. KEY PERSONNEL (MSA-1)

**9.1** Where a SOW identifies Key Personnel, Provider shall not replace, reassign, or remove Key Personnel from the engagement without first: (a) providing Client with not less than **twenty-one (21) calendar days'** prior written notice; (b) obtaining Client's prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed); and (c) ensuring a structured handover and knowledge transfer of at least **ten (10) business days** before the change takes effect.

**9.2** If Key Personnel become unavailable due to unforeseen circumstances (illness, resignation, termination), Provider shall: (a) notify Client within **two (2) business days**; (b) propose a replacement of equivalent or superior skill and experience within **five (5) business days**; and (c) undertake a knowledge transfer to the replacement as soon as practicable.

**9.3** If Client reasonably objects to a proposed replacement and the Parties are unable to agree on a suitable replacement within **fifteen (15) business days**, either Party may terminate the relevant SOW on **thirty (30) days'** written notice without penalty.

---

## 10. BACKGROUND VERIFICATION (MSA-2)

**10.1** Provider shall conduct background verification checks on all personnel assigned to provide Services under this Agreement, including Key Personnel and other staff with access to Client systems or data, in accordance with applicable Indian law.

**10.2** Background checks shall include, at minimum: (a) identity verification against government-issued documents; (b) employment history verification for the preceding five (5) years; (c) educational qualification verification; and (d) criminal record check, where permissible under applicable law and with the individual's written consent.

**10.3** Provider shall retain records of background checks for the term of this Agreement plus three (3) years, and shall make such records available to Client on written request, subject to applicable data protection laws.

**10.4** All background verification activities shall be conducted in compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and any other applicable Indian law governing the collection and processing of personal information.

---

## 11. CONFIDENTIALITY

**11.1** Each Party agrees to hold the other Party's Confidential Information in strict confidence, use it solely for the purposes of this Agreement, and not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except to Representatives who need to know for the purposes of this Agreement and are bound by equivalent confidentiality obligations.

**11.2** Confidentiality obligations survive termination of this Agreement for a period of **three (3) years** from the date of disclosure of the relevant Confidential Information.

**11.3** On termination, each Party shall return or destroy the other Party's Confidential Information as directed by the Disclosing Party, and certify such return or destruction in writing within ten (10) business days.

---

## 12. DATA PROTECTION — DPDP ACT 2023 (M-3)

**Data Protection and Privacy — DPDP Act 2023.**

- (a) **Applicability.** Applies to the extent either party processes Personal Data in connection with this Agreement.
- (b) **Compliance.** Each party shall comply with all applicable provisions of the DPDP Act, 2023 and rules thereunder, as amended.
- (c) **Data Processor Obligations.** Where Provider processes Personal Data as Data Processor, it shall: (i) process only per Client's documented lawful instructions; (ii) implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures per Section 8 of the DPDP Act; (iii) not engage sub-processors without Client's prior written authorisation; (iv) notify Client within seventy-two (72) hours of any Personal Data Breach; (v) assist with Data Principal rights including access, correction, erasure, and grievance redressal; (vi) on termination, delete or return all Personal Data unless retention is required by law; and (vii) permit audits on reasonable notice to demonstrate compliance.
- (d) **Consent.** Where processing requires consent under the DPDP Act, Client as Data Fiduciary shall obtain valid, informed, specific consent from Data Principals.
- (e) **Cross-Border Transfers.** Personal Data shall not be transferred outside India except to countries notified by the Central Government under Section 16 of the DPDP Act.
- (f) **Survival.** Obligations survive termination for as long as either party retains Personal Data in connection with this Agreement.

---

## 13. AUDIT RIGHTS (M-6)

**13.1 Records.** Each party shall maintain complete and accurate records relating to this Agreement for at least **seven (7) years** from creation, or longer as required by applicable law.

**13.2 Right to Audit.** On not less than **fifteen (15) business days'** written notice, and no more than **once per calendar year** (unless a prior audit revealed a material discrepancy), Client may audit Provider's records directly related to Provider's obligations under this Agreement, including records relating to Service Fees, service levels, and DPDP Act compliance.

**13.3 Conduct of Audit.** Audits shall be conducted: (a) during Provider's normal business hours; (b) by Client or a mutually agreed independent auditor bound by written confidentiality obligations no less stringent than this Agreement; (c) without causing unreasonable disruption to Provider's business; and (d) at Client's expense — unless the audit reveals underpayment or non-compliance of five percent (5%) or more, in which case Provider shall bear Client's reasonable audit costs.

---

## 14. WARRANTIES

**14.1 Provider Warranties.** Provider warrants that: (a) it has full legal capacity to enter into and perform this Agreement; (b) it holds all licences, consents, and authorisations required to provide the Services; (c) the Services will be performed with reasonable skill and care by suitably qualified personnel; (d) Deliverables will conform to the specifications set out in the relevant SOW; and (e) the Services and Deliverables will not infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party.

**14.2 Client Warranties.** Client warrants that: (a) it has full legal capacity to enter into this Agreement; (b) all information and materials provided to Provider are accurate and complete; and (c) Client has all rights and permissions necessary to provide its materials to Provider for use in performing the Services.

**14.3 Warranty Remedy.** If any Deliverable fails to conform to the applicable specifications within **thirty (30) days** of delivery, Client shall notify Provider in writing and Provider shall, at its sole cost, correct or re-perform the non-conforming Deliverable within a mutually agreed timeframe. If Provider fails to remedy the defect within such timeframe, Client may terminate the relevant SOW and receive a pro-rata refund of fees paid for the non-conforming Deliverable.

---

## 15. TERM AND TERMINATION

**15.1 Term.** This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for **[INITIAL_TERM] years**, unless terminated earlier in accordance with this clause. This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive periods of **one (1) year** unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal at least **ninety (90) days** before expiry of the then-current term.

**15.2 Termination for Convenience.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement (but not any active SOW) for convenience on **sixty (60) days'** written notice. Individual SOWs may only be terminated for convenience if expressly permitted in the relevant SOW.

**15.3 Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW immediately on written notice if: (a) the other Party commits a material breach and fails to cure such breach within **thirty (30) days** of written notice identifying the breach in reasonable detail; (b) the other Party becomes insolvent, is subject to liquidation, receivership, or insolvency proceedings; or (c) the other Party ceases to carry on business.

**15.4 Effects of Termination.** On termination: (a) each Party shall return or destroy the other's Confidential Information; (b) Client shall pay all outstanding Service Fees for Services performed up to the date of termination; (c) Provider shall deliver all Deliverables (or partially completed Deliverables) to Client; and (d) clauses that by their nature survive termination shall continue in full force.

**15.5 Survival.** Clauses 7, 8, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15.4, 16, 17, 18, 19, and 20 survive termination of this Agreement.

---

## 16. FORCE MAJEURE (M-5)

**16.1** Neither party shall be liable for delay or failure to perform (other than payment obligations) caused by a Force Majeure Event: acts of God, natural disasters, fire, government-declared pandemic, war, terrorism, civil unrest, acts of government or regulatory authorities, nationwide strikes beyond the party's control, third-party telecom or power failure, or court orders.

**16.2 Exclusions.** Economic downturns, market conditions, loss of revenue, inability to pay money when due, foreseeable technical failures, or staffing shortages from management decisions are NOT Force Majeure Events.

**16.3 Notice and Mitigation.** The Affected Party shall notify in writing within five (5) business days of becoming aware, use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate, and provide regular status updates.

**16.4 Extended Events.** If the event continues for sixty (60) consecutive days, either party may terminate on thirty (30) days' written notice without liability, except for amounts due for Services already performed.

---

## 17. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY (M-8)

**(a) Exclusion of Consequential Damages.** EXCEPT AS SET OUT IN CLAUSE 17(c), NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, GOODWILL, DATA, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY, HOWEVER ARISING.

**(b) Aggregate Cap.** EXCEPT AS SET OUT IN CLAUSE 17(c), TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY OF EITHER PARTY SHALL NOT EXCEED: (i) WHERE THE AGREEMENT HAS BEEN IN FORCE FOR TWELVE (12) OR MORE MONTHS — FEES PAID IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM; OR (ii) WHERE THE AGREEMENT HAS BEEN IN FORCE FOR LESS THAN TWELVE (12) MONTHS — TOTAL FEES PAID FROM THE EFFECTIVE DATE TO THE DATE OF THE CLAIM.

**(c) Unlimited Liability.** Clauses 17(a) and 17(b) do NOT apply to: (i) death or personal injury from negligence; (ii) fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; (iii) IP indemnification obligations under Clause 8; (iv) DPDP Act obligations including Personal Data Breach liability; (v) wilful misconduct or gross negligence; or (vi) liability that cannot be excluded under applicable Indian law.

**(d) Duty to Mitigate.** Each party shall take all reasonable steps to mitigate any loss and shall promptly notify the other party of any circumstance likely to give rise to a claim.

---

## 18. ELECTRONIC EXECUTION AND DIGITAL SIGNATURES (M-2)

Electronic signatures are valid, binding, and enforceable under the Information Technology Act, 2000 (as amended 2008) and the IT (Certifying Authorities) Rules, 2000. A digitally signed copy transmitted electronically is treated as an original and is admissible as evidence in any legal proceeding. Neither party shall contest the validity of this Agreement solely on grounds of electronic execution. Digital signatures shall comply with Schedule II of the IT Act, 2000. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each an original, all constituting one instrument.

---

## 19. GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION (M-1)

**(a) Negotiation.** In the event of any Dispute, the Parties shall first attempt resolution through good faith negotiation between senior representatives for thirty (30) days from written notice ("Negotiation Period").

**(b) Arbitration.** If unresolved after the Negotiation Period, the Dispute shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 (as amended 2021). Sole arbitrator mutually appointed within fifteen (15) days, failing which appointed by the High Court of Karnataka. Seat and venue: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. Language: English. Award: final and binding.

**(c) Interim Relief.** Either party may seek interim or injunctive relief from courts in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India, to prevent irreparable harm pending arbitration. This does not waive the right to arbitrate.

**(d) Exclusive Jurisdiction.** For matters not subject to arbitration, each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts at Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

**(e) Costs.** Each party bears its own dispute resolution costs unless the arbitral tribunal or court determines otherwise.

---

## 20. GENERAL PROVISIONS

**20.1 Entire Agreement.** This Agreement, together with all SOWs and Schedules, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior understandings, negotiations, and representations.

**20.2 Amendments.** No amendment is effective unless in writing and signed by authorised representatives of both Parties.

**20.3 Notices.** All notices shall be in writing, delivered by email with read receipt or by registered post to the addresses in Schedule 1.

**20.4 No Waiver.** Failure to exercise any right is not a waiver of that right.

**20.5 Severability.** If any provision is invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in full force.

**20.6 Assignment.** Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party. Any purported assignment without consent is void.

**20.7 Relationship.** The Parties are independent contractors. This Agreement does not create a partnership, joint venture, agency, or employment relationship.

**20.8 Subcontracting.** Provider may subcontract any part of the Services only with Client's prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Provider remains primarily liable for all subcontracted Services.
